scope.dev_files_modified_source: orchestrator --changed-files parameter; verified against `git diff HEAD --name-only`
scope.tagging_mechanism: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging with introduced_in_diff per AC-12.1 default-safe rule. Every finding carries an explicit introduced_in_diff:true|false boolean.
scope.default_safe_contract: Findings with introduced_in_diff:true AND blocks_close:true may force CLOSE: NO at the close.md verdict layer. Findings with introduced_in_diff:false are pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking per Section 5.4 rule 3. Untagged/absent defaults to ignore (none in this report — all 4 findings carry explicit tags).
scope.ground_truth_method: Read tool against current working-tree state at audit time, cross-checked with git blame to date each finding's source line to either this cycle's diff or a prior commit.

findings.description: TestReadGrant class-level docstring at L113 still says '"""Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_or_substr semantics)."""' but the read_grant() target the class exercises now uses exact_only literal_policy (cycle Item D). Two new test methods added INSIDE this class by THIS diff (test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_substring, test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_write_against_todowrite) explicitly assert exact_only behavior, making the class docstring directly contradict the tests it now contains. git blame attributes L113 to an earlier commit, but the contradiction is diff-introduced because the cycle's own additions are what created the mismatch — under AC-12.1 inspector-side tagging the cycle owns this.
findings.proposed_fix: Change L113 to: '"""Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only read-only semantics)."""'
findings.rationale_non_blocking: Documentation accuracy issue inside a test-file class docstring. Does not affect functional correctness, hook behavior, test discovery, or any acceptance criterion (10/10 ACs PASS per QA). Cosmetic — not a CLOSE: NO blocker per Section 5.4 cleanliness scope. Codex concurred.

findings.line_ref: L416-L417 docstring backref + L440-L442 NOTE comment block
findings.description: Stale _resolve_context cleanup breadcrumb in two adjacent locations: (a) docstring of _resolve_base_context at L416-417 reads 'replaces the previous _resolve_context gate-and-bail pattern for the legacy path' — but _resolve_context no longer exists in the file (its body was removed in a prior cleanup iteration of this same cycle); (b) a 3-line NOTE comment at L440-442 documents that a prior cleanliness-inspector finding was applied. Once the cycle closes, both references become dead-comment noise pointing at a symbol that does not exist. Per codex review, merged into a single finding (same symbol, same cycle, same fix).
findings.proposed_fix: Two-part edit: (a) reword the docstring sentence at L416-417 to drop the deleted symbol name, e.g. 'Cycle 20260519-211515 Item H: replaces the previous gate-and-bail pattern for the legacy path.'; (b) delete the 3-line NOTE comment block at L440-442 entirely — it documented a fix that is now landed and is therefore archaeology, not running documentation.
findings.rationale_non_blocking: Documentation/comment-only cleanup. No executable code involved. Does not affect any acceptance criterion. Cosmetic — not a CLOSE: NO blocker. Codex agreed on severity and recommended combining drafts into one finding (applied here).

findings.description: test_exact_match_returns_true creates a tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) at L120-L123 and never deletes it. The local variables sid and proper_path extracted at L124 and L126 are immediately discarded — L128-129 overwrite both with a controlled test_sid / grant_path pair. The NamedTemporaryFile leaks to /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-rg1.*.json on every test run (codex verified live leftover files dated through 2026-05-20). git blame attributes L119-127 to commit ea02581f (2026-05-18 17:27, prior cycle), so this is PRE-EXISTING — not introduced by this cycle's diff.
findings.proposed_fix: Delete the entire NamedTemporaryFile block at L119-L127 (everything from `with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(` through `proper_path = f"/tmp/..."`). The `# Use a controlled SID` branch at L128 onward is the actual test setup and is sufficient on its own.
findings.rationale_non_blocking: introduced_in_diff:false → per AC-12.1 default-safe rule this finding is advisory/non-blocking by definition. Pre-existing /tmp leak; cleanliness-relevant but not this cycle's responsibility. Surfaced for the next cleanup cycle to address.

findings.description: Two imports are present but never referenced in the test file body: (a) MagicMock at L14 ('from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock') — only patch is used (L102); MagicMock has zero references in the file. (b) MatchResult at L21 (in the lib.allowlist import block) — never referenced in any test method. git blame attributes both to commit ea02581f (2026-05-18, prior cycle), so this is PRE-EXISTING.
findings.proposed_fix: L14: change `from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock` → `from unittest.mock import patch`. L21: drop the `MatchResult,` line from the multi-line import block.
findings.rationale_non_blocking: introduced_in_diff:false → per AC-12.1 default-safe rule, advisory/non-blocking. Pre-existing unused-import warnings that ruff/pyflakes would flag in a regular lint pass; surfaced for the next style/lint cleanup cycle.

summary.estimated_space_saved: negligible (~15 lines comment/docstring + ~9 lines test boilerplate + 2 import names)

verdict_input_for_close.explanation: Zero major/critical findings. Zero findings with both introduced_in_diff:true AND blocks_close:true. All 4 findings are minor cosmetic/documentation issues. F1 and F2 are NEW-in-diff (cycle's diff is the proximate cause) but blocks_close:false (documentation-only). F3 and F4 are pre-existing (introduced_in_diff:false) and therefore advisory-only per AC-12.1 default-safe rule. Recommended close.md verdict input: CLEAN — proceed to CLOSE: YES. The 4 minor findings may either be fixed-forward in a follow-up cleanup cycle or left as documented advisories; none threaten the D+H acceptance criteria (10/10 ACs PASS per QA). Note: the prior iteration's _resolve_context dead-function finding was already addressed — the function body has been removed; only the stale breadcrumb (F2) remains.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 findings: (1) _resolve_context dead, (2) TestReadGrant docstring stale, (3) Path B header missing typed-lookup wording, (4) exact_or_substr branch in allowlist.py production-orphan, (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY clean naming. Findings 1 and 2 were verified and applied. Finding 3 was rejected after direct file read showed the Path B comment (L507-L510) already contains the typed-lookup precedence and no-fallthrough mandate codex suggested adding. Finding 4 was rejected as library-API design preservation: exact_or_substr is a generic policy option in _match_loaded_grant exercised by tests; the branch is not orphan code but a preserved API surface. Finding 5 was logged as observation_only.

findings.issue: _resolve_context() in hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py is now unreferenced after _main() switched to _resolve_base_context() and duplicated strict contract gating. Newly dead private helper code.
findings.proposed_fix: Remove _resolve_context() entirely; keep the 'strict contract gate' explanation in _main() if needed.
findings.rationale: Verified via git show HEAD vs current file grep: function had one caller pre-cycle (line 372 of _main()), now zero callers. Cycle's diff is the proximate cause. Flagged in dead_functions with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:true.

findings.issue: TestReadGrant class docstring still says 'exact_or_substr semantics' but read_grant() now uses exact_only.
findings.proposed_fix: Change to: '"""Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only read-only semantics)."""'
findings.rationale: Verified via grep: line 113 docstring contradicts the cycle's two new tests added inside the class. Although the diff did not literally touch line 113, the inconsistency is diff-introduced. Flagged in naming_violations with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:false (cosmetic). Codex's proposed wording adopted verbatim.

findings.issue: Path B header in hooks/posttool-subagent-track.py omits typed-lookup precedence / no-fallthrough wording.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace Path B paragraph with wording stating typed-lookup precedence and no-fallthrough on miss.
findings.rationale: Direct file read of lines 507-510 shows the Path B comment ALREADY contains the wording codex proposed: 'Case A (typed lookup via subagent_type) takes precedence over Case B (legacy in_progress inference). The two are mutually exclusive; Case A MUST NOT fall through to Case B on a typed miss (AC-H3b mandate).' Codex's read of the file was incomplete; the diff already addresses this concern.

findings.issue: exact_or_substr branch in allowlist.py is no longer production-called after read_grant() switched to exact_only; only tests call that helper policy.
findings.proposed_fix: Refactor bash branch 1 to call _match_loaded_grant with exact_or_substr, or delete the helper policy and tests.
findings.rationale: exact_or_substr is a generic literal_policy option in _match_loaded_grant — a library-API surface, not caller-specific dead code. It IS exercised by tests (test_exact_or_substr_exact_match, test_exact_or_substr_substring_match in TestMatchLoadedGrant). Deletion would be a breaking API change to a freshly-stabilized hook lib. The branch remains a valid future-caller option. This is library-design preservation, not orphan code. Out of scope for cleanliness inspector.

findings.issue: New helper/test naming and reference status look clean.
findings.rationale: _resolve_base_context, _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step, _extract_subagent_type are all called by _main() in the same file. New test method names are valid snake_case and unittest-discoverable. No new TODO/FIXME/legacy markers introduced by the diff. Confirmed via grep.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex Finding 1 (_resolve_context dead) applied as dead_functions finding with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:true. Codex Finding 2 (TestReadGrant stale docstring) applied as naming_violations finding with introduced_in_diff:true and blocks_close:false (cosmetic). Codex Finding 3 rejected with file-read evidence. Codex Finding 4 rejected as library-API preservation. Codex Finding 5 logged as observation_only.
